items.title: Ora
items.text:
Praying the Rosary and traditional Christian prayers as a path to language learning.

This project revives the ancient practice of the Rosary for learners of Latin and other languages. The prayers are presented primarily in Latin with translations to aid memorization and understanding.

items.title: The Rosary
items.text:
The Rosary is a scriptural prayer that meditates on the life of Christ through the eyes of Mary.

How to pray the Rosary

items.title: Prayers
items.text:
Core prayers used in the Rosary:

  • Sign of the Cross
  • Our Father
  • Hail Mary
  • Glory Be

Additional prayers and full mysteries will be added as the site grows.

items.title: Languages
items.text: Content focuses on Latin (the traditional language of the Church) with English and Spanish translations to begin with. More languages coming.

items.title: How to Pray the Rosary
items.text: The Rosary is prayed using a string of beads while meditating on mysteries from the life of Jesus and Mary.

items.title: Basic Steps
items.text:
1. Make the Sign of the Cross.
  2. Pray the Apostles' Creed.
  3. Pray one Our Father.
  4. Pray three Hail Marys.
  5. Pray the Glory Be and Fatima Prayer.
  6. Announce the first mystery and pray the decade (Our Father + 10 Hail Marys + Glory Be).
  7. Repeat for 5 decades (one set of mysteries).
  8. Conclude with the Hail Holy Queen and Sign of the Cross.

See the individual prayers for the texts.

items.title: The Mysteries
items.text:
The Rosary is divided into sets of mysteries:

  • Joyful Mysteries
  • Sorrowful Mysteries
  • Glorious Mysteries
  • Luminous Mysteries (added by Pope John Paul II)

Sample structure will be expanded with dedicated pages.

items.title: The Prayers of the Rosary
items.text:
These are the core prayers recited during the Rosary.

Each prayer is shown in Latin (traditional), with English and Spanish for language learning.

  • Sign of the Cross
  • Our Father
  • Hail Mary
  • Glory Be

More prayers (Apostles' Creed, Fatima Prayer, Hail Holy Queen) will follow.

items.title: Glory Be (Gloria Patri)
items.text: A short doxology praising the Trinity.

items.title: Latin
items.text: Gloria Patri, et Filio, et Spiritui Sancto. Sicut erat in principio, et nunc et semper, et in saecula saeculorum. Amen.

items.title: English
items.text: Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit. As it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen.

items.title: Spanish
items.text: Gloria al Padre, y al Hijo, y al Espíritu Santo. Como era en el principio, ahora y siempre, por los siglos de los siglos. Amén.

items.title: Notes for Language Learners
items.text: This prayer is very stable across languages and excellent for pronunciation practice of Latin endings (-o, -um, etc.).

items.title: Hail Mary (Ave Maria)
items.text: The central prayer of the Rosary, combining Scripture and tradition.

items.title: Latin
items.text: Ave Maria, gratia plena, Dominus tecum. Benedicta tu in mulieribus, et benedictus fructus ventris tui, Iesus. Sancta Maria, Mater Dei, ora pro nobis peccatoribus, nunc et in hora mortis nostrae. Amen.

items.title: English
items.text: Hail Mary, full of grace, the Lord is with thee. Blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb, Jesus. Holy Mary, Mother of God, pray for us sinners, now and at the hour of our death. Amen.

items.title: Spanish
items.text: Dios te salve, María, llena eres de gracia, el Señor es contigo. Bendita tú eres entre todas las mujeres, y bendito es el fruto de tu vientre, Jesús. Santa María, Madre de Dios, ruega por nosotros pecadores, ahora y en la hora de nuestra muerte. Amén.

items.title: Notes for Language Learners
items.text: "Ave" is the Latin greeting. "Gratia plena" = full of grace. Compare to Spanish "llena eres de gracia".

items.title: Our Father (Pater Noster)
items.text: The prayer taught by Jesus himself.

items.title: Latin
items.text: Pater noster, qui es in caelis, sanctificetur nomen tuum. Adveniat regnum tuum. Fiat voluntas tua, sicut in caelo et in terra. Panem nostrum quotidianum da nobis hodie, et dimitte nobis debita nostra sicut et nos dimittimus debitoribus nostris. Et ne nos inducas in tentationem, sed libera nos a malo. Amen.

items.title: English
items.text: Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name; thy kingdom come, thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven. Give us this day our daily bread, and forgive us our trespasses, as we forgive those who trespass against us; and lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. Amen.

items.title: Spanish
items.text: Padre nuestro, que estás en el cielo, santificado sea tu nombre; venga a nosotros tu reino; hágase tu voluntad en la tierra como en el cielo. Danos hoy nuestro pan de cada día; perdona nuestras ofensas, como también nosotros perdonamos a los que nos ofenden; no nos dejes caer en la tentación, y líbranos del mal. Amén.

items.title: Notes for Language Learners
items.text: The Latin version is the root for many Romance languages. Compare "Pater" (father) to "Padre".

items.title: Sign of the Cross
items.text: The Sign of the Cross is made at the beginning and end of the Rosary and most prayers.

items.title: Latin (Traditional)
items.text: In nomine Patris, et Filii, et Spiritus Sancti. Amen.

items.title: English
items.text: In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.

items.title: Spanish
items.text: En el nombre del Padre, y del Hijo, y del Espíritu Santo. Amén.

items.title: Practice Tip
items.text: Trace a cross on your body while reciting. This physical act helps with memorization across languages.
